acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Local and remote ingest paths persist events and watermarks atomically in one SQLite transaction
- [ ] No code path can compile if it records event rows without producing watermark updates in the same durable commit type
- [ ] Crash-window regression test proves restart replay cannot miss committed events
description:
**Problem**
`executor` and `repl_ingest` commit WAL-index event rows and watermarks in separate SQLite transactions. A crash between commits can persist events while leaving durable/applied watermarks stale, so restart replay skips committed events.

**Files:** `crates/beads-daemon/src/runtime/executor.rs`, `crates/beads-daemon/src/runtime/core/repl_ingest.rs`, `crates/beads-daemon/src/runtime/core/helpers.rs`, `crates/beads-daemon-core/src/wal/index.rs`
design: Introduce a single typed commit object for durable mutation writes (events + segments + HLC + watermarks) so split-commit is unrepresentable. Eliminate public APIs that allow watermark commit in a second transaction for mutation paths.
title: Atomic WAL index writes for events + watermarks
